channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Hombres, escondan a sus gatos
item.description: O, al menos, no los pongan en la foto de su perfil de citas en línea.
item.dc:creator: Michael Zadoorian

media:content.media:credit: Brian Rea

item.title: Paul McCartney ya no necesita hacer música. Pero le encanta
item.description: Con un nuevo disco que recupera sus raíces en Liverpool y los métodos analógicos de los Beatles, McCartney demuestra que la música nunca fue un trabajo: es una adicción.
item.dc:creator: Jon Pareles

media:content.media:credit: Jim Dyson/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Paul McCartney en una presentación en Londres en 2024.

item.title: Un suplemento de longevidad podría estar basado en un principio erróneo
item.description: Una nueva investigación plantea que la idea que impulsó el consumo de NAD+ como suplemento contra el envejecimiento puede no ser precisa.
item.dc:creator: Dana G. Smith

media:content.media:credit: Ghazalle Badiozamani para The New York Times

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for May 30, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Inside Humorist David Sedaris’s Two Upper East Side Apartments
item.description: The humorist on art collecting, interacting with fans and a surprising upside of the Upper East Side.
item.dc:creator: Addie Morfoot

item.title: ‘Partying With a Purpose’: The Dance Party Making Millions for Parks
item.description: Concerts under the Kosciuszko Bridge in north Brooklyn have drawn more than 350,000 people since 2022 and raised millions to maintain local parks and open spaces.
item.dc:creator: Winnie Hu and Anna Connors
item.category: Open Space Alliance for North Brooklyn

media:content.media:credit: Anna Connors for The New York Times

item.title: His Opponents Keep Talking About His Past Ties to a Militant Cleric
item.description: Dr. Adam Hamawy, a New Jersey Democrat with big-name support on the left, has touted his humanitarian work, but some want to talk about a person he met in 1991.
item.dc:creator: Tracey Tully

media:content.media:credit: Hannah Beier for The New York Times

item.title: ¿Es normal babear mientras duermo?
item.description: La saliva es importante para nuestra salud, pero incluso un pequeño goteo puede ser embarazoso. Los expertos explican por qué ocurre.
item.dc:creator: Simar Bajaj

media:content.media:credit: Eric Helgas para The New York Times

item.title: White House Releases Results of Trump’s Latest Physical Exam
item.description: The report by President Trump’s physician said he was in “excellent health.” It showed that he had gained weight and that neurological and heart tests had come back “normal.”
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron
item.category: Reed, Walter, National Military Medical Center

media:content.media:credit: Jose Luis Magana
media:content.media:description: President Trump at the White House last week. The results of his recent physical exam were released late Friday.

item.title: Lo que 370.000 ensayos universitarios nos dicen sobre los efectos de la IA en la creatividad
item.description: La IA puede ser una muleta que perjudique nuestra capacidad de pensar creativamente.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Winthrop

item.title: How Curry Shops Got Caught in Japan’s Immigration Crackdown
item.description: Under Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi, new visa rules are forcing some foreign business owners, who have put down roots in Japan, to leave.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández and Kiuko Notoya

media:content.media:credit: Noriko Hayashi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mahendra Dharmapriya at his Sri Lankan restaurant in Shimotsuke, Japan.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Invasive Species? A Top Flower Show Buzzes Over Gnomes.
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Saturday, May 30, 2026.

item.title: Lithuania, Once Occupied by Germany, Is Glad German Troops Are Back
item.description: Berlin has deployed forces to protect the Baltic country amid fears of increased Russian aggression in Europe. This time, the soldiers were welcomed.
item.dc:creator: Christopher F. Schuetze

media:content.media:credit: Andrej Vasilenko for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: German soldiers on their base in Vilnius, Lithuania, in May. The forces deployed in the Baltic country reflect Germany’s increased willingness to act as Europe’s shield amid wavering support from the United States.

item.title: Who’s the Vegas Retiree With the Big Offer for Greenland?
item.description: Clifford E. Stanley, a retired broker, explains his unusual mission, which caught a lot of people by surprise.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Gettleman and Maya Tekeli

media:content.media:credit: Oscar Scott Carl for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Clifford E. Stanley, a retired mortgage broker, traveled to Nuuk, Greenland, to make residents an offer.

item.title: Corrections: May 31, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Sunday, May 31, 2026.

item.title: Corrections: May 30, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Saturday, May 30, 2026.

item.title: China’s Rise in Drug Development Looms Over U.S.
item.description: Clinical trials in China are getting attention at an international oncology gathering in Chicago. China’s surging biotechnology industry is fueling alarm that U.S. dominance in the field is waning.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Robbins and Gina Kolata

media:content.media:credit: Zach Boyden-Holmes/ASCO
media:content.media:description: The American Society of Clinical Oncology annual meeting in Chicago last year. One of the conference’s coveted headliners this year will be a presentation of a clinical trial conducted only in China.

item.title: As Climate Change Extends Europe’s Heat Season, Schools Bake
item.description: Classrooms can be particularly vulnerable amid climate change, as the hottest times of the year increasingly overlap with the academic calendar.
item.dc:creator: Chico Harlan and Léontine Gallois

media:content.media:credit: Philippe Lopez/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Classes ended early at a school in Soustons, France, on Thursday amid the European heat wave.

item.title: Among Mamdani’s Priorities, Economic Development Seems Low on the List
item.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani has not filled the vacant role of president of the Economic Development Corporation, deepening concern over his attention to the New York City economy.
item.dc:creator: Dana Rubinstein, Sally Goldenberg, Jeffery C. Mays and Emma Goldberg

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s team has considered at least 10 people as they look to hire a president of the city’s Economic Development Corporation.

item.title: Rolling in It
item.description: Malaika Handa and Erik Agard present a puzzle that’s dripping with great details.
item.dc:creator: Caitlin Lovinger

media:content.media:credit: George Rose/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Time to crack open a beverage on your outside love seat and enjoy a cool themeless puzzle.

item.title: Homelessness Declined in 2024, According to Delayed Federal Report
item.description: The modest drop marked the first decrease in homelessness in nearly a decade. The Housing and Urban Development report was published months later than usual.
item.dc:creator: Jason DeParle

media:content.media:credit: Ariana Drehsler for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A homeless encampment in Los Angeles. Though homelessness declined in 2024, it is still at unusually high levels by historical standards.

item.title: Trump Lifts Restrictions on Off-Road Vehicles on Public Lands
item.description: The president rescinded two executive orders that led to prohibitions on the use of the vehicles in most national parks.
item.dc:creator: Maxine Joselow

media:content.media:credit: Tamir Kalifa for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A horse crosses a road in Big Bend National Park in Texas in March.

item.title: El Senado de México vota a favor de permitir que las autoridades anulen elecciones por ‘injerencia extranjera’
item.description: La legislación, que llega en medio de crecientes tensiones entre México y la Casa Blanca, ahora debe ser aprobada por la mayoría de las legislaturas estatales y enviada a la presidenta.
item.dc:creator: Emiliano Rodríguez Mega

media:content.media:credit: Fred Ramos para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Votaciones en Ciudad de México en 2024.

item.title: Judge Orders Removal of Trump’s Name From Kennedy Center
item.description: A federal judge in Washington on Friday ordered that President Trump’s name be removed from the facade of the John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts.
item.dc:creator: Jackeline Luna
item.category: Kennedy, John F, Center for the Performing Arts

media:content.media:credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson/Associated Press

item.title: Iran’s Hard-Liners Try to Derail Potential Deal With the U.S.
item.description: A political fight is playing out in Iran, where the small but loud faction of hard-liners has used rallies, state media and private and public statements to try to undermine negotiations.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A billboard of the late supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, and his successor, Mojtaba Khamenei, during a ceremony in Tehran on Sunday.

item.title: ICE Protesters Convicted in a Spokane Case That Tested a Trump Strategy
item.description: The three demonstrators in Spokane, Wash., had been accused of conspiracy, charges that the region’s former top prosecutor has said were unwarranted.
item.dc:creator: Anna Griffin

media:content.media:credit: Rajah Bose for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Downtown Spokane, Wash., in 2022. The city’s mayor called the verdict in the protesters’ case “politically motivated.”

item.title: How Olivia Rodrigo’s Love Songs Became a Breakup Album
item.description: The pop star Olivia Rodrigo spoke to “Popcast” about the writing process for her new album, “you seem pretty sad for a girl so in love.”
item.dc:creator: Popcast

item.title: ICE Agents to Leave Site of Volatile Protests at Detention Center
item.description: Clashes between protesters and armed federal agents have erupted at the parking lot of Delaney Hall in Newark since the Memorial Day weekend.
item.dc:creator: Ana Ley and Mark Bonamo

media:content.media:credit: Todd Heisler/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Federal agents and demonstrators who were protesting conditions inside the Delaney Hall detention center in Newark have faced off for nearly a week.

item.title: Actually, Most Immigrants Won’t Need to Leave U.S. to Get Green Cards, D.H.S. Says
item.description: But details remained scant after officials said last week that, with “extraordinary” exceptions, people seeking permanent residency must first leave the country.
item.dc:creator: Hamed Aleaziz, Madeleine Ngo and Lydia DePillis

media:content.media:credit: Valerie Plesch for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Department of Homeland Security appeared to make a partial walk-back of a change announced last Friday.

item.title: Trump Endorses Pamela Evette in South Carolina Governor’s Race
item.description: Pamela Evette had been among the most vocal proponents of redrawing the state’s congressional map to help Republicans in the midterms.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Bogel-Burroughs and Emily Cochrane

media:content.media:credit: Meg Kinnard/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: President Trump on Friday endorsed South Carolina’s lieutenant governor, Pamela Evette, to be the state’s next governor.

item.title: Justice Department Lurches Toward Trump’s Goals
item.description: Plus, the Kennedy Center must remove Trump’s name. Here’s the latest at the end of Friday.
item.dc:creator: Evan Gorelick

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: E. Jean Carroll in 2024 after a hearing on one of her lawsuits.

item.title: Canadian Man Pleads Guilty to Aiding 14 Suicides
item.description: Kenneth Law, who ran an online business that shipped toxic salt to customers in 40 countries, also admitted to causing the deaths of 79 people in Britain, prosecutors said.
item.dc:creator: Michael Levenson

media:content.media:credit: Arlyn Mcadorey/The Canadian Press, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Kenneth Law, a Canadian man accused of running businesses that shipped toxic salt to people in dozens of countries, pleaded guilty on Friday to 14 counts of aiding suicide in Ontario, prosecutors said.

item.title: What We Know (and Don’t) About the E. Jean Carroll Lawsuits Inquiry
item.description: Prosecutors would face substantial hurdles in potentially pursuing charges against Ms. Carroll, who twice won cases against Donald Trump, or the billionaire who helped pay her lawyers.
item.dc:creator: Adam Liptak and Benjamin Weiser

media:content.media:credit: Jefferson Siegel for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: E. Jean Carroll arrived for closing arguments in her defamation trial against Donald Trump in Manhattan in 2023.

item.title: Judge Reopens Trump’s Lawsuit Demanding $10 Billion From IRS
item.description: The ruling was a blow to both President Trump, who had voluntarily dismissed the suit last week, and to the Justice Department, which used the suit to establish a fund likely intended for Trump allies.
item.dc:creator: Alan Feuer and Andrew Duehren

media:content.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: In a stern order on Friday, a judge said that she wanted to investigate the circumstances surrounding President Trump’s efforts to settle the lawsuit in a way that benefited him and his allies.

item.title: Can Talarico win over some of Cornyn’s voters in Texas?
item.description: Democrats hope so, but there are good reasons to be skeptical.
item.dc:creator: Katie Glueck

media:content.media:credit: Danielle Villasana/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: James Talarico

item.title: Free Bus From Skin Care Brand the Ordinary Connecting Domino and Prospect Parks Stops Running
item.description: A two-week marketing stunt by the Ordinary appeared to stop abruptly on Friday, stranding riders.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher

media:content.media:credit: via The Ordinary
media:content.media:description: The Ordinary’s bus was meant to run between Williamsburg and Prospect Park for two weeks.

item.title: Bondi, Pressed Over Epstein Files, Places Responsibility on Blanche and Patel
item.description: Her remarks, delivered during a closed-door interview before the House Oversight Committee, were a candid admission of her own powerlessness.
item.dc:creator: Glenn Thrush and Michael Gold

media:content.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Former Attorney General Pam Bondi arrives for the House Oversight Committee closed hearing on Friday.

item.title: Mexican Senate Votes to Allow Voiding Elections Over Foreign Interference
item.description: The legislation, which comes amid growing tensions between Mexico and the White House, must be approved by a majority of state legislatures and sent to the president.
item.dc:creator: Emiliano Rodríguez Mega

media:content.media:credit: Fred Ramos for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Voting in Mexico City in 2024.

item.title: You Be the Ump
item.description: Major league umpires are facing video review on balls and strikes for the first time. How would you do?
item.dc:creator: Michael Beswetherick and Sean Catangui

item.title: The Confusion Around Modern Masculinity
item.description: In the wake of #MeToo, changing workplace norms left many men unsure of how to behave. But was that confusion genuine — or just an excuse? On “The Opinions,” Frederick Joseph argues that many men became “childlike during that time.”

item.title: Surveillance Ordered for Hantavirus Ship Passengers Who Go Home
item.description: Trump administration officials want local health authorities to constantly monitor the 18 passengers for another three weeks, a requirement that far exceeds typical protocols.
item.dc:creator: Jacey Fortin and Apoorva Mandavilli
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

media:content.media:credit: Dylan Widger/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: The passengers who have been housed in the National Quarantine Unit at the University of Nebraska Medical Center were aboard a cruise ship that became the center of a global hantavirus outbreak this month.

item.title: What This Pixar Film Gets Wrong About Boys
item.description: Films like “Inside Out” raise questions about how we portray boys’ emotional lives. “It just felt like every time that a male character appeared onscreen in that movie, they were an emotional idiot,” Ruth Whippman says on “The Opinions.”

item.title: Claude Bessy, Who Ruled Paris Opera Ballet School, Dies at 93
item.description: A tall, commanding ballerina, she led one of the world’s top dance institutions for decades, though her rigorous methods eventually drew criticism.
item.dc:creator: Adam Nossiter

media:content.media:credit: Roger Viollet, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Claude Bessy in 1957.

item.title: Rescuers Free Miner Trapped Inside a Cave in Laos
item.description: One of the miners trapped inside a cave for more than a week was freed on Friday. Rescue crews are still working to pull out four more miners and find two others who are missing.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Mitssunaga

media:content.media:credit: Association Of Volunteers For Lao People, via Associated Press

item.title: What’s Next for Blue Origin After Rocket Explosion
item.description: Jeff Bezos was gaining ground on Elon Musk’s SpaceX and Starlink. Thursday’s rocket explosion on a launchpad creates a major setback.
item.dc:creator: Karen Weise

media:content.media:credit: Nasaspaceflight.com, via Reuters
media:content.media:description: Blue Origin’s New Glenn rocket exploded on its launchpad during a test.

item.title: Journalist Favored by Trump Is in Talks to Join White House in Temporary Role
item.description: John Solomon said he was being vetted for a role as a “special government employee,” a status given to part-time, outside advisers to the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Maggie Haberman and Luke Broadwater
item.category: Russian Interference in 2016 US Elections and Ties to Trump Associates
Federal Criminal Case Against Trump (Documents Case)

media:content.media:credit: Celal Gunes/Anadolu Agency, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: The journalist John Solomon spoke at the Conservative Political Action Conference in Maryland in 2023.

item.title: Sugar Cookie Bars
item.description: These sugar cookie bars, which are adapted from “American Girl Cookies,” are happiness in a 9-by-13 pan according to Margaux Laskey, a recipes editor for NYT Cooking. The addition of cream cheese in the batter makes them very tender and slightly tangy, a perfect counterpoint to the sweet buttercream frosting.

media:content.media:credit: David Malosh for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Simon Andrews.

item.title: Basil Tofu
item.description: Inspired by the savory heat of pad krapow, the popular Thai dish of stir-fried ground chicken or pork with chiles, garlic and basil, this tofu version is just as robustly seasoned and satiating.

media:content.media:credit: Julia Gartland for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Samantha Seneviratne.

item.title: Coconut Saag
item.description: Saag paneer is a classic North Indian dish — but it’s also endlessly riffable. This is a particularly stellar riff, in which coconut milk enriches an already aromatic and verdant sauce that can be paired with either the traditional paneer, or extra-firm tofu.

media:content.media:credit: Kelly Marshall for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Samantha Seneviratne. Prop Stylist: Paige Hicks.

item.title: Cottage Cheese Egg Bites
item.description: Egg bites are a perfect, protein-rich snack to have on hand for busy mornings. Blending eggs with cottage cheese and shredded cheese, dividing the mixture among muffin cups and baking in a water bath result in a soft, delicate texture.

media:content.media:credit: Ghazalle Badiozamani for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Barrett Washburne.
